name: YouTube
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of YouTube — the world's video-hosting platform whose interface is the most-viewed brand surface on the internet. Canvas is light-mode-white or dark-mode near-black — both rendered as default options at user choice. The signature is the YouTube red (#ff0000) used only on the play-button glyph, the wordmark, and the 'Subscribe' button. Type runs Roboto (the Google default) at modest scale. The interface is dominated by the video-thumbnail grid: 3-up at desktop, each thumbnail with a 16:9 aspect ratio, duration badge in the corner, channel avatar circle, and a 2-line title. The brand voice is 'the channel is the brand' — YouTube itself stays invisible while creator content carries every visual moment.

design_language.motion_signature: Hover on video thumbnail auto-plays a 6-second muted preview at 1x scale (no transform). Subscribe-button toggle animates with a satisfying 120ms color-fill from red to gray. The play-button overlay scales 1.1x on hover at 100ms ease-out. Page transitions are fast: under 200ms. The brand voice is 'utility motion' — never decorative, always responsive.

trademark_signals: Play-button rectangle — the red rounded-rectangle with the white triangle — the most-recognized streaming-platform glyph on the internet
Video-thumbnail grid at 3-up desktop with 16:9 thumbnails, duration badge bottom-right, channel avatar circle, 2-line title, channel name, view count, time-since-upload
Dual canvas (light + dark) as a user-preference — both ship as default options; the brand renders identically in both modes
Roboto at every text tier — refusing custom typography in favor of Google's house grotesk
Circular channel avatars — every channel identity rendered as a 32px or 88px circle, never a square, never a banner — the channel-avatar circle IS the creator's identity unit
ai_slop_avoided: Don't substitute the play-button rounded-rect with a circular play button. The rounded-rect-with-triangle is the brand's most-recognized glyph
Don't drop the duration badge from thumbnails. The bottom-right time-overlay is a recognized YouTube affordance
Don't replace Roboto with a custom face. YouTube's typographic restraint IS the brand voice — custom typography reads as off-brand
Don't restyle channel avatars to squares or banners. The 32px or 88px circular avatar is the creator-identity unit
Don't paint the canvas with brand red. YouTube red is reserved for the play-button glyph and the 'Subscribe' button — using it as a section background reads as off-brand
